---
title: "LRPITT11: ITT analysis of nonword reading (N) — floor-rule branch"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

If the model was not fitted in "reporting" mode, the results will be more approximate than where additional sampling has been performed.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import arviz as az
import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
trace = az.from_netcdf(_here / "trace.nc")
tau_offfloor = pd.read_csv(_here / "tau_summary.csv")  # PRIMARY (binary off-floor)
movers = pd.read_csv(_here / "offfloor_movers.csv")
tau_graded = pd.read_csv(_here / "tau_summary_graded.csv")  # SECONDARY (graded)
zero_ppc = pd.read_csv(_here / "proportion_at_zero_ppc.csv")
```

## Overview

LRPITT11 is the intention-to-treat (ITT) analysis for nonword reading
(`nonword`). The estimand is the total effect of randomised group assignment $G$
on the outcome, using the RCT phase only (time 1 $\rightarrow$ time 2). Nonword
reading is the repo's "post-only" measure and is heavily floored at t2 (~72% of
post-scores at zero), so it takes the pre-specified **floor-rule branch** (#119).

::: {.callout-important title="Floored, post-only outcome — the binary off-floor estimand is PRIMARY"}

An outcome with $\geq 40\%$ of its post-scores at zero at t2 (computed **arm-blind**
and fixed **before** fitting) takes the floor rule. This model:

- uses an **age-only** predictor (no own baseline); the four children with a
  missing t1 nonword baseline are kept, since the age-only model never uses that
  (degenerate) baseline — it is the baseline, not the outcome, that is missing;
- reports the **binary "off-floor at t2"** effect, $\Pr(\text{post} > 0)$, as the
  **PRIMARY** estimand; and
- retains the graded Beta-Binomial $\tau$ only as a flagged, **detection-limited
  SECONDARY**, read alongside the per-arm mover counts and never on its own.

:::

::: {.callout-note title="Identification and sign convention"}

Under the locked causal DAG, $G$ is a randomised root, so the ITT effect $\tau$ is
identified by the **empty adjustment set**; linear age is a precision term only.
**Sign convention:** $G = 1$ is the intervention arm, so a **positive** $\tau$ means
the intervention raises the outcome — here, raises the probability of coming off
the floor.

:::

## Model

PRIMARY (binary off-floor), with $\eta_i = \alpha + \tau\,G_i + \gamma_A\,A_{\text{std},i}$:

$$
[\,N_{\text{post},i} > 0\,] \sim \text{Bernoulli}\big(\sigma(\eta_i)\big).
$$

SECONDARY (graded, detection-limited): the *same* age-only $\eta_i$ with a
Beta-Binomial likelihood on the count, $N_{\text{post},i} \sim \text{BetaBinomial}(N, \mu_i\kappa, (1-\mu_i)\kappa)$, $N = 6$.

![Model diagram (primary)](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Shared priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.svg){width=32%}
![tau](prior_tau.svg){width=32%}
![gamma_age](prior_gamma_age.svg){width=32%}

(The primary off-floor model has no own-baseline or Beta-Binomial concentration
term; `gamma_own`/`kappa` enter only the graded secondary.)

## Sampling diagnostics (primary)

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Posterior](posterior_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Off-floor treatment effect (PRIMARY)

```{python}
# | echo: false
tau_offfloor
```

- `tau_logit_*`: posterior mean and equal-tailed central interval (coverage `ci_prob`) of $\tau$ on the **log-odds-of-off-floor** scale.
- `tau_prob_*`: the average marginal effect on the **probability of coming off the floor** (risk difference), per posterior draw.
- `prob_tau_pos`: $P(\tau > 0) = P(\text{intervention raises off-floor probability})$.

### Off-floor movers by arm

```{python}
# | echo: false
movers
```

## Proportion-at-zero posterior predictive check

Does the graded Beta-Binomial reproduce the observed floor? (Typically not — the
motivation for the binary primary estimand.)

![Proportion at zero PPC](proportion_at_zero_ppc.png){.lightbox}

```{python}
# | echo: false
zero_ppc
```

## Graded Beta-Binomial effect (SECONDARY)

::: {.callout-warning title="Detection-limited — read only beside the mover counts"}

The graded $\tau$ below is reported for completeness only. With most post-scores at
the floor it is leveraged by a few dispersed tail values, not by the arm contrast;
interpret it solely alongside the off-floor primary and the per-arm mover table.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
tau_graded
```

## Posterior predictive check (primary)

![Posterior predictive](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## LOO-PSIS (primary)

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
